---
title: "René Canales"
subtitle: |
  PhD Fellow \
  [Bremen International Graduate School of Social Sciences (BIGSSS)](https://www.bigsss-bremen.de/academic-program/daad-graduate-school-scholarship-program/copodi-research-and-faculty) \
description-meta: "René Canales is a Sociologist from the Pontifical Catholic University of Chile and holds an M.A. in Sociology from the same institution. Currently, he is a PhD fellow at the Bremen International Graduate School of Social Sciences (BIGSSS). His research focuses on contentious politics, social stratification, political attitudes, and institutional change, using quantitative methods that combine observational data, causal inference, computational methods and survey experiments."
image: "files/images/rene_canales_profile.JPG"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
page-layout: full
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 80%
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:rene.canales@uc.cl
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/renejcanales
    - icon: orcid
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0002-4299-2725
      target: _blank
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=m9aAaWUAAAAJ&hl=es
      target: _blank
---


::: {#hero-heading .justify}

Hello, I’m René Canales Sellés. I am a sociologist from the Pontifical Catholic University of Chile and hold an M.A. in Sociology from the same institution.

Currently, I am a PhD fellow at the [Bremen International Graduate School of Social Sciences (BIGSSS)](https://www.bigsss-bremen.de/) in the [CoPoDi Research Program](https://www.bigsss-bremen.de/academic-program/daad-graduate-school-scholarship-program/copodi-research-and-faculty), at the University of Bremen, Germany. 

My research focuses on the relationship between social movements and political attitudes, with a particular interest in how protest and contentious politics shape social cohesion, public opinion and influence institutional change. Methodologically, I primarily use quantitative approaches, combining observational data with causal inference strategies, survey experiments and computational methods.

Previously, I worked as a research assistant for the [Social Cohesion Observatory (OCS-COES)](https://ocs-coes.com/) and the [Laboratory of Social Open Science](lisacoes.cl), both at the [Center of Studies of Social Conflict and Social Cohesion (COES)](https://coes.cl/). In both individual and collaborative research, I value reproducible practices based on Git, R, and other open-source tools.

I was born and raised in [Santiago de Chile](https://youtu.be/E4bxx1nRjCg?si=8q8ZuL2jKgHspUvp). Outside academia, I enjoy reading, powerlifting, listening to music and attending concerts and festivals.

:::

## Recent Papers

Check my latest [papers](publications.qmd#category=papers) and [projects](publications.qmd#category=book-chapters) in the [Research](publications.qmd) section.
